issues.comment: ErrDeviceLimitReached error is returned when rowsAffected == 0, but this happens both when device doesn't exist AND when device exists but is outside the time window, making the error message misleading and harder to debug.

TagDevice call changed from asynchronous (goroutine with timeout) to synchronous execution, which will block authentication and could cause performance degradation during high load or database delays.

CountDevices check and subsequent updateDevice call are not atomic - between counting devices and calling updateDevice, another request could create additional devices, potentially exceeding the limit, though this may be acceptable for approximate limiting.

Test sets UpdatedAt to time.Now().Add(-time.Hour) (1 hour ago) but in real usage UpdatedAt should be current time when creating/updating devices, making the test not representative of actual behavior.
Test creates Device without setting CreatedAt field, leaving it as zero value, which may not match real usage where CreatedAt should be set to a valid timestamp.
